Central Question: How did Nick move from lifelong obesity, repeated weight regain, unhealthy coping habits, and extreme exercise toward sustainable weight maintenance?
Central Struggle: Years of overeating, excessive drinking, compulsive gaming, crash dieting, weight cycling, emotional avoidance, and temporary goals prevented lasting change.

Episode Title: How I overcame emotional & binge eating
Transcript Filename: EP-005 - How I overcame emotional & binge eating - EP 005.txt

Central Question: How can someone interrupt emotional eating and binge eating by understanding what drives the behavior and changing what happens before and after a binge?
Central Struggle: Using food to escape stress, anxiety, boredom, dissatisfaction, and fear of regain, while restriction and intermittent fasting intensify hunger and create repeated binge cycles.
Core Coaching Theme: Identify the life emotions behind the urge, document how foods and binges affect you, pause before acting, and avoid compensatory restriction by returning to regular nourishing meals after a binge.
Emotional Themes: emotional eating, binge eating, intermittent fasting, OMAD, food journal, binge cycle, overeating
Target Audience: shame, anxiety, boredom, loneliness, fear, self-awareness, hope, self-trust

Hidden Concepts: avoidance coping, emotional regulation, interoceptive awareness, self-monitoring, cognitive reframing, delayed response
Myths Debunked: Emotional eating is only a food problem; intermittent fasting works for everyone; the correct response to a binge is to skip meals the next day; having an emotional connection to food means you are an emotional eater; one binge removes your ability to choose differently.
Key Takeaways: Emotional eating often uses food to escape problems elsewhere in life, while binge eating can be intensified by prolonged restriction and extreme hunger.
